This package contains functions and procedures for managing the stack for
the Lisp interpreter.

record_types.name: stack_entry

tagged_types.name: lisp_stack
tagged_types.code: type lisp_stack(size : Natural) is tagged private;

documentation.description:
Procedure for clearing stack.  This is done at the command line level.
  There should be nothing on the stack at this point.  Some error conditions
  may cause a return to the command line without clearing the stack.

subprograms.name: find_offset
subprograms.code: function find_offset(self : lisp_stack; name : string_index; index : out Natural) return Natural

documentation.description: Returns the frame pointer so that it can be private.

subprograms.name: get_entry
subprograms.code: function get_entry(self : in out lisp_stack; e : Natural; err : out Boolean) return stack_entry

documentation.description: Sets an entry on the stack
Adding and removing items from the stack

subprograms.name: push
subprograms.code: procedure push(self : in out lisp_stack; name : string_index; val : element_type; err : out Boolean)

documentation.description:
Operations for stack frames.  The usage is as follows:
  1) Call start_frame before pushing items onto the stack that should be
     in the frame.
  2) Call enter_frame once the items are all on the stack.  This finalizes
     stack frame creation.
  3) Call exit_frame to clean up the stack frame.  There is no need to pop
     the items off the stack that belong to the stack frame.

subprograms.name: search_frames
subprograms.code: function search_frames(self : lisp_stack; offset : Natural; name : string_index) return element_type

documentation.description:
Search stack for the variable.  The frame offset and name are used to
  look backwards through the stack frames for a match to the name.  If
  found, the stack index of the variable is returned, if not 0 is returned.

subprograms.name: search_frames
subprograms.code: function search_frames(self : lisp_stack; offset : Natural; name : string_index) return Natural

documentation.description: Searches the stack to find a variable and returns the stack index and offset

subprograms.name: set_entry
subprograms.code: procedure set_entry(self : in out lisp_stack; e : Natural; v : stack_entry; err : out Boolean)

documentation.description: Sets the value of an entry on the stack

subprograms.name: set_value
subprograms.code: procedure set_value(self : in out lisp_stack; e : Natural; v : element_type; err : out Boolean)

documentation.description: Gets an entry from the stack

subprograms.name: start_frame
subprograms.code: procedure start_frame(self : in out lisp_stack; err : out Boolean)

documentation.description:
Procedure for clearing stack.  This is done at the command line level.
  There should be nothing on the stack at this point.  Some error conditions
  may cause a return to the command line without clearing the stack.
